title: To Warren Hastings, Esq. By An Old Schoolfellow Of His At Westminster.
content:
Hastings! I knew thee young, and of a mind,
    While young, humane, conversable, and kind,
    Nor can I well believe thee, gentle then,
    Now grown a villain, and the worst of men.
    But rather some suspect, who have oppress�d
    And worried thee, as not themselves the best.
meaning:
This evocative piece by William Cowper, titled "To Warren Hastings, Esq. By An Old Schoolfellow Of His At Westminster.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
